summary: Software developer with hands-on experience building and deploying AI-powered applications, integrating LLMs, REST APIs, and full-stack pipelines into production systems. Comfortable across the full software development lifecycle including containerization, CI/CD automation, database design, and cloud deployment. Eager to contribute an AI-native mindset to a growth-stage HR tech product focused on intelligent recruitment automation.

skills.ml: LLM Integration (Claude, GPT-4o), LangChain, RAG Systems, HuggingFace, DistilBERT, XGBoost, YOLO, SHAP, Deepgram, OpenAI Embeddings, Pinecone, Vector Search
skills.infra: Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions (CI/CD), Railway, Supabase (PostgreSQL), GCP, Git, REST APIs, Agile workflows

experience.title: Deep Learning Intern
bullets: Containerized a computer vision model with Docker and deployed on Kubernetes for horizontal scaling of model inference, enabling the team to handle variable load without code changes, applying software development lifecycle practices in a production environment.

Developed a YOLO-based object detection model in Python to detect and classify industrial pipe inventory from warehouse images, improving accuracy by 30% and eliminating a fully manual counting process.
Designed normalized relational database schemas in SQL to store image metadata and inference results, enabling cross-team queries on model performance and inventory trends.

Built a data augmentation pipeline using NumPy and Pandas, scaling the training dataset from a few hundred to 10,000+ images and manually re-annotating 500+ edge cases; accuracy improved from 65% to 85.4% on held-out evaluation data.

projects.name: AI Interview Engine
projects.subtitle: Voice-First Candidate Screening Platform
projects.stack: Next.js, Express, Claude Sonnet/Haiku, Deepgram, Supabase, Railway, Vercel
bullets: Built an automated document ingestion pipeline (PDF parsing, Claude Haiku, MD5 deduplication caching) and a post-interview evaluation system scoring candidates across 4 dimensions with advance/hold/reject output; deployed full-stack on Railway, Vercel, and Supabase (PostgreSQL plus Row-Level Security plus Magic Link authentication) with Brevo SMTP for transactional email automation.

Designed a multi-turn conversational AI engine with dynamic context injection: the LLM receives each candidate's parsed resume before the session and generates targeted, role-specific follow-up questions in real time, directly applicable to AI recruitment agent products.

Engineered a real-time voice pipeline integrating Deepgram Nova-2 (speech-to-text) and Aura (text-to-speech) with Claude Sonnet 4.6 (large language model), achieving 4-5s end-to-end latency per conversational turn and replacing human interviewers for initial candidate screening.

projects.name: CodeSyntax AI
projects.subtitle: Full-Stack RAG Developer Assistant
projects.stack: Python, FastAPI, Next.js, LangChain, Pinecone, Docker, GitHub Actions
bullets: Maintained 35+ pytest unit tests and a 55-question domain evaluation set; GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline builds the Docker image and deploys to Railway (API) and Vercel (frontend) on every merge to main, demonstrating disciplined software development lifecycle and testing practices.

Built a production retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system using GPT-4o-mini with semantic search over Python Docs, RealPython, and StackOverflow, indexed in Pinecone via OpenAI vector embeddings, exposing the system through a RESTful FastAPI backend.

Implemented an LLM-based query intent classifier and result re-ranker by source authority, plus multi-turn conversation support via LangChain chat-history condensing, both measurably reducing off-topic responses.

projects.name: AuthentiText
projects.subtitle: AI-Generated Text Detection Engine
projects.stack: Python, XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, GCP, Docker
bullets: Trained a weighted ensemble model combining XGBoost (21 hand-crafted linguistic features: perplexity, burstiness, lexical density) with a fine-tuned DistilBERT transformer, achieving 99.1% F1 and 99.96% AUROC on held-out test data.

Conducted systematic model comparison across four architectures (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, DistilBERT) using ROC curves, confusion matrices, and SHAP feature importance; integrated Captum token-level attribution heatmaps for explainable AI (XAI).
Trained on the merged HC3 corpus spanning Wikipedia, Reddit, medicine, and finance domains; deployed as a publicly accessible machine learning inference app on HuggingFace Spaces using Docker on GCP.
